    10→func CreateEnrollmentAfterFirstTouch(d *sql.DB, opts EnrollmentOptions) (*Enrollment, *Task, error) {
    11→	if opts.MailboxID == "" {
    12→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("mailbox ID is required")
    13→	}
    14→	if opts.ContactEmail == "" {
    15→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("contact email is required")
    16→	}
    17→	if opts.SentAt.IsZero() {
    18→		opts.SentAt = time.Now()
    19→	}
    20→	if existing, err := ActiveEnrollmentForContact(d, opts.MailboxID, opts.ContactEmail); err != nil {
    21→		return nil, nil, err
    22→	} else if existing != nil {
    23→		return nil, nil, fmt.Errorf("%s already has an active sequence enrollment (%s)", opts.ContactEmail, existing.ID)
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	template, err := TemplateByName(d, opts.SequenceName)
    27→	if err != nil {
    28→		return nil, nil, err
    29→	}
    30→
    31→	tx, err := d.Begin()
    32→	if err != nil {
    33→		return nil, nil, err
    34→	}
    35→	defer tx.Rollback()
    36→
    37→	enrollmentID := newID("seq")
    38→	nextDue := ""
    39→	var nextTask *Task
    40→	if len(template.Steps) > 1 {
    41→		nextDelay, err := stepDelay(template.Steps[1])
    42→		if err != nil {
    43→			return nil, nil, err
    44→		}
    45→		nextDueAt := opts.SentAt.Add(nextDelay).UTC()
    46→		nextSubject, nextBody := renderStep(template.Steps[1], Enrollment{
    47→			SequenceName: template.Name,
    48→			CampaignName: defaultCampaignName(opts),
    49→			MailboxID:    opts.MailboxID,
    50→			ContactEmail: opts.ContactEmail,
    51→			ContactName:  opts.ContactName,
    52→			Subject:      opts.Subject,
    53→			ThreadID:     opts.ThreadID,
    54→			InitialBody:  opts.InitialBody,
    55→		})
    56→		nextDue = nextDueAt.Format(time.RFC3339)
    57→		nextTask = &Task{
    58→			ID:           newID("task"),
    59→			EnrollmentID: enrollmentID,
    60→			MailboxID:    opts.MailboxID,
    61→			ContactEmail: opts.ContactEmail,
    62→			Subject:      nextSubject,
    63→			Body:         nextBody,
    64→			ThreadID:     opts.ThreadID,
    65→			StepNumber:   template.Steps[1].StepNumber,
    66→			StepName:     template.Steps[1].Name,
    67→			SendType:     template.Steps[1].SendType,
    68→			Status:       TaskStatusOpen,
    69→			DueAt:        nextDueAt.Format(time.RFC3339),
    70→			SequenceName: template.Name,
    71→		}
    72→	}
    73→
    74→	_, err = tx.Exec(`
    75→		INSERT INTO sequence_enrollments (
    76→			id, sequence_name, campaign_name, mailbox_id, contact_email, contact_name, subject,
    77→			thread_id, last_message_id, status, status_reason, reply_state, current_step, total_steps, last_sent_at, last_reply_at, next_due_at, initial_body
    78→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    79→		enrollmentID, template.Name, defaultCampaignName(opts), opts.MailboxID, opts.ContactEmail, opts.ContactName,
    80→		opts.Subject, opts.ThreadID, opts.MessageID, StatusActive, "", "", 1, len(template.Steps),
    81→		opts.SentAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), "", nextDue, opts.InitialBody,
    82→	)
    83→	if err != nil {
    84→		return nil, nil, err
    85→	}
    86→
    87→	if nextTask != nil {
    88→		_, err = tx.Exec(`
    89→			INSERT INTO follow_up_tasks (
    90→				id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, contact_email, subject, body, thread_id,
    91→				step_number, step_name, send_type, status, send_status, due_at
    92→			) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
    93→			nextTask.ID, nextTask.EnrollmentID, nextTask.MailboxID, nextTask.ContactEmail,
    94→			nextTask.Subject, nextTask.Body, nextTask.ThreadID, nextTask.StepNumber, nextTask.StepName,
    95→			nextTask.SendType, nextTask.Status, TaskSendStatusPending, nextTask.DueAt,
    96→		)
    97→		if err != nil {
    98→			return nil, nil, err
    99→		}
   100→		if err := UpsertDraftForTask(tx, nextTask.ID, nextTask.EnrollmentID, nextTask.StepNumber, nextTask.Subject, nextTask.Body, "sequence-runtime", "initial enrollment"); err != nil {
   101→			return nil, nil, err
   102→		}
   103→	}
   104→
   105→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   106→		return nil, nil, err
   107→	}
   108→
   109→	enrollment, err := EnrollmentByID(d, enrollmentID)
   110→	if err != nil {
   111→		return nil, nil, err
   112→	}
   113→
   114→	if nextTask != nil {
   115→		nextTask, err = TaskByID(d, nextTask.ID)
   116→		if err != nil {
   117→			return nil, nil, err
   118→		}
   119→	}
   120→
   121→	return enrollment, nextTask, nil
   122→}

   124→func AdvanceTaskAfterSend(d *sql.DB, opts AdvanceOptions) (*Task, error) {
   125→	if opts.TaskID == "" {
   126→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("task ID is required")
   127→	}
   128→	if opts.SentAt.IsZero() {
   129→		opts.SentAt = time.Now()
   130→	}
   131→
   132→	task, err := TaskByID(d, opts.TaskID)
   133→	if err != nil {
   134→		return nil, err
   135→	}
   136→	if task.Status != TaskStatusOpen {
   137→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("task %s is %s", task.ID, task.Status)
   138→	}
   139→
   140→	enrollment, err := EnrollmentByID(d, task.EnrollmentID)
   141→	if err != nil {
   142→		return nil, err
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	template, err := TemplateByName(d, enrollment.SequenceName)
   146→	if err != nil {
   147→		return nil, err
   148→	}
   149→
   150→	tx, err := d.Begin()
   151→	if err != nil {
   152→		return nil, err
   153→	}
   154→	defer tx.Rollback()
   155→
   156→	_, err = tx.Exec(`
   157→		UPDATE follow_up_tasks
   158→		SET status = ?, send_status = ?, completed_at = ?, updated_at = datetime('now')
   159→		WHERE id = ?`,
   160→		TaskStatusCompleted, TaskSendStatusSent, opts.SentAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), task.ID,
   161→	)
   162→	if err != nil {
   163→		return nil, err
   164→	}
   165→	if err := UpdateDraftStatusByTaskID(tx, task.ID, "sent"); err != nil {
   166→		return nil, err
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	if task.StepNumber >= len(template.Steps) {
   170→		_, err = tx.Exec(`
   171→			UPDATE sequence_enrollments
   172→			SET status = ?, current_step = ?, last_sent_at = ?, next_due_at = '',
   173→				mailbox_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN mailbox_id ELSE ? END,
   174→				thread_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN thread_id ELSE ? END,
   175→				last_message_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN last_message_id ELSE ? END,
   176→				updated_at = datetime('now')
   177→			WHERE id = ?`,
   178→			StatusCompleted, task.StepNumber, opts.SentAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
   179→			opts.MailboxID, opts.MailboxID,
   180→			opts.ThreadID, opts.ThreadID, opts.MessageID, opts.MessageID, enrollment.ID,
   181→		)
   182→		if err != nil {
   183→			return nil, err
   184→		}
   185→
   186→		if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   187→			return nil, err
   188→		}
   189→		return nil, nil
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	nextStep := template.Steps[task.StepNumber]
   193→	nextDelay, err := stepDelay(nextStep)
   194→	if err != nil {
   195→		return nil, err
   196→	}
   197→	nextDueAt := opts.SentAt.Add(nextDelay).UTC()
   198→	nextTaskID := newID("task")
   199→	nextMailboxID := chooseString(opts.MailboxID, enrollment.MailboxID)
   200→	nextThreadID := chooseString(opts.ThreadID, enrollment.ThreadID)
   201→	renderEnrollment := *enrollment
   202→	renderEnrollment.MailboxID = nextMailboxID
   203→	renderEnrollment.ThreadID = nextThreadID
   204→	renderEnrollment.LastMessageID = chooseString(opts.MessageID, enrollment.LastMessageID)
   205→	nextSubject, nextBody := renderStep(nextStep, renderEnrollment)
   206→
   207→	_, err = tx.Exec(`
   208→		INSERT INTO follow_up_tasks (
   209→			id, enrollment_id, mailbox_id, contact_email, subject, body, thread_id,
   210→			step_number, step_name, send_type, status, send_status, due_at
   211→		) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)`,
   212→		nextTaskID, enrollment.ID, nextMailboxID, enrollment.ContactEmail,
   213→		nextSubject, nextBody, nextThreadID, nextStep.StepNumber,
   214→		nextStep.Name, nextStep.SendType, TaskStatusOpen, TaskSendStatusPending, nextDueAt.Format(time.RFC3339),
   215→	)
   216→	if err != nil {
   217→		return nil, err
   218→	}
   219→	if err := UpsertDraftForTask(tx, nextTaskID, enrollment.ID, nextStep.StepNumber, nextSubject, nextBody, "sequence-runtime", "advance task"); err != nil {
   220→		return nil, err
   221→	}
   222→
   223→	_, err = tx.Exec(`
   224→		UPDATE sequence_enrollments
   225→		SET current_step = ?, last_sent_at = ?, next_due_at = ?,
   226→			mailbox_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN mailbox_id ELSE ? END,
   227→			thread_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN thread_id ELSE ? END,
   228→			last_message_id = CASE WHEN ? = '' THEN last_message_id ELSE ? END,
   229→			updated_at = datetime('now')
   230→		WHERE id = ?`,
   231→		task.StepNumber, opts.SentAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), nextDueAt.Format(time.RFC3339),
   232→		nextMailboxID, nextMailboxID,
   233→		opts.ThreadID, opts.ThreadID, opts.MessageID, opts.MessageID, enrollment.ID,
   234→	)
   235→	if err != nil {
   236→		return nil, err
   237→	}
   238→
   239→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   240→		return nil, err
   241→	}
   242→
   243→	return TaskByID(d, nextTaskID)
   244→}

   246→func EnrollmentByID(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) (*Enrollment, error) {
   247→	row := d.QueryRow(`
   248→		SELECT
   249→			e.id, e.sequence_name, e.campaign_name, e.mailbox_id, m.email, e.contact_email,
   250→			e.contact_name, e.subject, e.thread_id, e.last_message_id, e.status, e.status_reason,
   251→			e.reply_state, e.current_step, e.total_steps, e.last_sent_at, e.last_reply_at, e.next_due_at, e.initial_body
   252→		FROM sequence_enrollments e
   253→		JOIN mailboxes m ON m.id = e.mailbox_id
   254→		WHERE e.id = ?`,
   255→		enrollmentID,
   256→	)
   257→
   258→	var e Enrollment
   259→	if err := row.Scan(
   260→		&e.ID, &e.SequenceName, &e.CampaignName, &e.MailboxID, &e.MailboxEmail, &e.ContactEmail,
   261→		&e.ContactName, &e.Subject, &e.ThreadID, &e.LastMessageID, &e.Status, &e.StatusReason,
   262→		&e.ReplyState, &e.CurrentStep, &e.TotalSteps, &e.LastSentAt, &e.LastReplyAt, &e.NextDueAt, &e.InitialBody,
   263→	); err != nil {
   264→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   265→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("sequence enrollment %q not found", enrollmentID)
   266→		}
   267→		return nil, err
   268→	}
   269→	return &e, nil
   270→}
   271→
   272→func ListEnrollments(d *sql.DB, status string, limit int) ([]Enrollment, error) {
   273→	query := `
   274→		SELECT
   275→			e.id, e.sequence_name, e.campaign_name, e.mailbox_id, m.email, e.contact_email,
   276→			e.contact_name, e.subject, e.thread_id, e.last_message_id, e.status, e.status_reason,
   277→			e.reply_state, e.current_step, e.total_steps, e.last_sent_at, e.last_reply_at, e.next_due_at, e.initial_body
   278→		FROM sequence_enrollments e
   279→		JOIN mailboxes m ON m.id = e.mailbox_id`
   280→	var args []any
   281→	if status != "" {
   282→		query += " WHERE e.status = ?"
   283→		args = append(args, status)
   284→	}
   285→	query += " ORDER BY e.updated_at DESC"
   286→	if limit > 0 {
   287→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	rows, err := d.Query(query, args...)
   291→	if err != nil {
   292→		return nil, err
   293→	}
   294→	defer rows.Close()
   295→
   296→	var enrollments []Enrollment
   297→	for rows.Next() {
   298→		var e Enrollment
   299→		if err := rows.Scan(
   300→			&e.ID, &e.SequenceName, &e.CampaignName, &e.MailboxID, &e.MailboxEmail, &e.ContactEmail,
   301→			&e.ContactName, &e.Subject, &e.ThreadID, &e.LastMessageID, &e.Status, &e.StatusReason,
   302→			&e.ReplyState, &e.CurrentStep, &e.TotalSteps, &e.LastSentAt, &e.LastReplyAt, &e.NextDueAt, &e.InitialBody,
   303→		); err != nil {
   304→			return nil, err
   305→		}
   306→		enrollments = append(enrollments, e)
   307→	}
   308→
   309→	return enrollments, rows.Err()
   310→}
   311→
   312→func ActiveEnrollmentForContact(d *sql.DB, mailboxID, contactEmail string) (*Enrollment, error) {
   313→	row := d.QueryRow(`
   314→		SELECT id
   315→		FROM sequence_enrollments
   316→		WHERE mailbox_id = ? AND lower(contact_email) = lower(?) AND status IN (?, ?)
   317→		ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   318→		LIMIT 1`,
   319→		mailboxID, strings.TrimSpace(contactEmail), StatusActive, StatusPaused,
   320→	)
   321→
   322→	var enrollmentID string
   323→	if err := row.Scan(&enrollmentID); err != nil {
   324→		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
   325→			return nil, nil
   326→		}
   327→		return nil, err
   328→	}
   329→	return EnrollmentByID(d, enrollmentID)
   330→}
   331→
   332→func PauseEnrollment(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) error {
   333→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   334→		UPDATE sequence_enrollments
   335→		SET status = ?, updated_at = datetime('now')
   336→		WHERE id = ? AND status != ?`,
   337→		StatusPaused, enrollmentID, StatusCompleted,
   338→	)
   339→	return err
   340→}
   341→
   342→func ResumeEnrollment(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) error {
   343→	_, err := d.Exec(`
   344→		UPDATE sequence_enrollments
   345→		SET status = ?, updated_at = datetime('now')
   346→		WHERE id = ? AND status = ?`,
   347→		StatusActive, enrollmentID, StatusPaused,
   348→	)
   349→	return err
   350→}
   351→
   352→func ListLeads(d *sql.DB, limit int, includeSuppressed bool) ([]Lead, error) {
   353→	query := `
   354→		SELECT
   355→			c.email, MAX(c.name), MAX(c.domain), MAX(c.company), MAX(c.title), MAX(c.owner),
   356→			MAX(c.lead_status), MAX(COALESCE(c.last_activity_at, '')), MAX(c.email_count),
   357→			COALESCE(se.status, ''), COALESCE(se.sequence_name, ''), COALESCE(se.campaign_name, ''),
   358→			CASE WHEN se_sup.id IS NOT NULL OR sd_sup.id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END,
   359→			COALESCE(se_sup.reason, sd_sup.reason, '')
   360→		FROM contacts c
   361→		LEFT JOIN sequence_enrollments se
   362→			ON se.id = (
   363→				SELECT id FROM sequence_enrollments
   364→				WHERE contact_email = c.email
   365→				ORDER BY updated_at DESC
   366→					LIMIT 1
   367→				)
   368→		LEFT JOIN suppressions se_sup
   369→			ON se_sup.scope = 'email' AND se_sup.value = lower(c.email)
   370→		LEFT JOIN suppressions sd_sup
   371→			ON sd_sup.scope = 'domain' AND sd_sup.value = lower(c.domain)
   372→		GROUP BY c.email, se.status, se.sequence_name, se.campaign_name, se_sup.id, se_sup.reason, sd_sup.id, sd_sup.reason
   373→		ORDER BY MAX(COALESCE(c.last_activity_at, '')) DESC, c.email ASC`
   374→	if limit > 0 {
   375→		query += fmt.Sprintf(" LIMIT %d", limit)
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	rows, err := d.Query(query)
   379→	if err != nil {
   380→		return nil, err
   381→	}
   382→	defer rows.Close()
   383→
   384→	var leads []Lead
   385→	for rows.Next() {
   386→		var lead Lead
   387→		var suppressed int
   388→		if err := rows.Scan(
   389→			&lead.Email, &lead.Name, &lead.Domain, &lead.Company, &lead.Title, &lead.Owner,
   390→			&lead.LeadStatus, &lead.LastActivityAt, &lead.EmailCount,
   391→			&lead.EnrollmentStatus, &lead.SequenceName, &lead.CampaignName,
   392→			&suppressed, &lead.SuppressionReason,
   393→		); err != nil {
   394→			return nil, err
   395→		}
   396→		lead.Suppressed = suppressed == 1
   397→		if !includeSuppressed && lead.Suppressed {
   398→			continue
   399→		}
   400→		leads = append(leads, lead)
   401→	}
   402→
   403→	return leads, rows.Err()
   404→}

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary document of all the implementation boundaries I've found.

## Comprehensive Implementation Boundaries Report

Based on my exploration of the codebase, here are the concrete details of the existing interfaces and systems you need to integrate with:

---

### 1. **PIPELINE STATUS SYSTEM** (`internal/pipeline/status.go`)

**Struct Definition:**
```go
type PipelineStatus struct {
    GeneratedAt string                // RFC3339 timestamp
    NewOutreach NewOutreachStatus     // First-touch capacity
    FollowUps   FollowUpStatus        // Follow-up tracking
    Pipeline    PipelineFunnel        // Funnel stages
    Capacity    CapacityInfo          // Lead runway
    ByPersona   []PersonaPipeline     // Per-persona breakdown
    BySource    []SourcePipeline      // Per-source effectiveness
    Nudge       *SmartNudge          // Diagnostic recommendation
    LastSyncAt  string               // Last external sync
}
```

**Nudge Types (7 types):**
- `NudgeEmptyPipeline` - no leads
- `NudgeMessagingProblem` - 50+ sends, 0 replies
- `NudgeResearchBottleneck` - many sourced, few ready
- `NudgePoolDraining` - < 7 days of lead supply
- `NudgeCapacityWasted` - ready < daily limit
- `NudgePersonaImbalance` - persona pipeline empty
- `NudgeHealthy` - pipeline is good

**Data Pulled From:**
- `contacts` - sourced count
- `research_dossiers` - researched count
- `sequence_enrollments` - contacted/replied counts
- `reply_events` - replied count
- `mailbox_daily_usage` - sends by day
- `mailboxes` - daily caps
- `follow_up_tasks` - due/overdue counts

**Function Signature:**
```go
func Status(d *sql.DB, now time.Time) (*PipelineStatus, error)
func DiagnoseNudge(ps *PipelineStatus) *SmartNudge
```

---

### 2. **KNOWLEDGE ATOMS** (`internal/assembly/knowledge.go`)

**Atom Types (7 types):**
```go
const (
    AtomTone        = "tone"        // Voice/formality, refined by learning
    AtomHooks       = "hooks"       // Opener patterns (learned over time)
    AtomPositioning = "positioning" // Product pitch, pain points
    AtomPersonas    = "personas"    // Buyer personas (seeded at setup)
    AtomObjections  = "objections"  // Pushback handling (learned)
    AtomSignals     = "signals"     // Timing indicators (learned)
    AtomCompliance  = "compliance"  // Geo-legal requirements
)
```

**KnowledgeAtom Struct:**
```go
type KnowledgeAtom struct {
    ID         int              // auto-increment PK
    AtomType   AtomType         // one of 7 types
    Scope      string           // empty = global, or persona/segment name
    Content    json.RawMessage  // flexible JSON content
    Source     string           // "setup", "distill", etc.
    Confidence float64          // 1.0 = high, decays with age
    LastUsedAt string           // RFC3339
    UseCount   int              // incremented on touch
    CreatedAt  string
    UpdatedAt  string
}
```

**Table:** `knowledge_atoms`
- Unique constraint: `(atom_type, scope)`
- Operations: `SeedAtom()`, `QueryAtoms()`, `TouchAtom()`, `ListAtoms()`, `DeleteAtom()`

---

### 3. **SESSION LEARNING & DISTILLATION** 

#### Learning Types (`internal/session/distill.go`):
```go
type Learning struct {
    ID            int    // auto-increment
    Type          string // tone|icp|product|pattern|preference|competitor
    Content       string // free-form text
    SourceSession string // session ID where captured
    CreatedAt     string
}

// Learning type → atom type mappings:
var learningTypeToAtomTypes = map[string][]string{
    "tone":       {"tone"},
    "icp":        {"personas", "positioning"},
    "product":    {"positioning"},
    "pattern":    {"hooks", "tone"},
    "preference": {}, // handled specially
    // "competitor" → strategic_memory instead
}
```

**Distill Function Signature:**
```go
func Distill(db *sql.DB) (*DistillResult, error)

type DistillResult struct {
    LearningsProcessed int      
    AtomsUpdated       int      
    AtomsCreated       int      
    Categories         []string 
}
```

**How distillation works:**
1. Loads undistilled learnings from `learnings` table
2. Groups by type
3. Appends to atom `content` JSON under a "learnings" array
4. If no atoms exist, creates one with global scope
5. Marks learnings as distilled (sets `distilled_at` timestamp)

**Note:** The schema I reviewed doesn't show `distilled_at` column, but distill.go references it — likely needs schema update.

---

### 4. **SESSION HOOKS** (`cmd/hook/session.go` and `cmd/hook/banner.go`)

**SessionStart Hook** (`runSessionStart()`):
1. Reads stdin from Claude Code (session info)
2. Syncs permissions
3. Attempts session start sync (graceful, non-blocking)
4. Checks for auto-updates
5. Gathers status:
   - Config loaded state
   - Email stats (total, outbound)
   - This week's stats
   - Skills count
   - Contacts count
   - Learnings count
   - Recent strategic memory
   - Stale skills
   - Pipeline status
6. Outputs JSON with:
   - `SystemMessage` (visible banner with pipeline dashboard)
   - `AdditionalContext` (LLM prompt context)

**Banner includes:**
- Version info
- Pipeline dashboard (new outreach, follow-ups, funnel, sources)
- Nudge message if not healthy
- Sync status
- Stale skills refresh suggestions

**Prompt Context includes:**
- User context (name, role)
- Setup status
- Pipeline state
- Source effectiveness
- Per-persona pipeline
- Sync context
- Strategic memory + stale skills
- KPI context
- Sending context
- Geography/compliance notes
- Available skills

---

### 5. **EMAIL SYNC SYSTEM** (`internal/email/sync.go`)

**What it stores:**

```go
type RawMessage struct {
    ID          string // Gmail message ID
    Source      string // "gmail" or other
    Direction   string // "outbound" or "inbound"
    ThreadID    string
    Subject     string
    FromEmail   string
    ToEmails    string // JSON array
    CcEmails    string // JSON array
    Date        string // RFC3339
    Snippet     string
    BodyPreview string
    Labels      string // JSON array of label IDs
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Fetched  int           // messages seen
    Stored   int           // messages inserted
    Cursor   string        // sync cursor (RFC3339 timestamp)
    Duration time.Duration
}
```

**Tables:**
- `emails` - raw message storage
- `contacts` - created/updated from To/Cc/From addresses

**Sync Logic:**
- First run: fetches last 90 days of sent mail (`in:sent` query)
- Subsequent: uses cursor to fetch only new
- Upserts contacts for each recipient/sender
- Updates `connectors` table with cursor + total_synced

**Function Signatures:**
```go
func Sync(srv *gm.Service, d *sql.DB) (*SyncResult, error)
func StoreEmail(d *sql.DB, e *RawMessage) error
func StoreConvexEmails(d *sql.DB, emails []RawMessage) (*SyncResult, error)
```

---

### 6. **SEQUENCE SYSTEM** (`internal/sequence/`)

**Key Types:**

```go
type Enrollment struct {
    ID            string // seq:xxx
    SequenceName  string
    CampaignName  string
    MailboxID     string
    MailboxEmail  string
    ContactEmail  string
    Status        string // active|paused|completed|stopped
    CurrentStep   int    // 1-indexed
    TotalSteps    int
    LastSentAt    string
    LastReplyAt   string
    NextDueAt     string
    ThreadID      string
    LastMessageID string
    InitialBody   string
    ReplyState    string
}

type Task struct {
    ID            string // task:xxx
    EnrollmentID  string
    MailboxID     string
    ContactEmail  string
    StepNumber    int
    StepName      string
    SendType      string // first_touch|follow_up|reply|manual
    Status        string // open|completed|canceled
    SendStatus    string // pending|sent
    DueAt         string
    Body          string
}

type Template struct {
    Name  string         // sequence name
    Steps []TemplateStep
}

type TemplateStep struct {
    StepNumber      int
    Name            string
    SendType        string
    Delay           string // "72h", "1m", "0s"
    SubjectTemplate string
    BodyTemplate    string
}
```

**Status Constants:**
```go
const (
    StatusActive    = "active"
    StatusPaused    = "paused"
    StatusCompleted = "completed"
    StatusStopped   = "stopped"
    
    TaskStatusOpen      = "open"
    TaskStatusCompleted = "completed"
    TaskStatusCanceled  = "canceled"
    
    SendTypeFirstTouch = "first_touch"
    SendTypeFollowUp   = "follow_up"
    SendTypeReply      = "reply"
    SendTypeManual     = "manual"
)
```

**Built-in Templates:**
- `default-3-touch` (1st touch, 72h follow-up, 96h follow-up)
- `gentle-2-touch` (1st touch, 120h follow-up)
- `test-3-touch-1m` (for testing)

**Core Operations:**
```go
func CreateEnrollmentAfterFirstTouch(d *sql.DB, opts EnrollmentOptions) (*Enrollment, *Task, error)
func AdvanceTaskAfterSend(d *sql.DB, opts AdvanceOptions) (*Task, error)
func EnrollmentByID(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) (*Enrollment, error)
func ListEnrollments(d *sql.DB, status string, limit int) ([]Enrollment, error)
func ActiveEnrollmentForContact(d *sql.DB, mailboxID, contactEmail string) (*Enrollment, error)
func PauseEnrollment(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) error
func ResumeEnrollment(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) error
```

**Tables:**
- `sequence_enrollments` - active sequences per contact
- `follow_up_tasks` - scheduled follow-ups
- `sequence_templates` - template definitions
- `sequence_drafts` - pre-generated follow-up content
- `campaigns` - campaign metadata
- `suppressions` - domain/email blocklists
- `reply_events` - inbound replies tracked by enrollment
- `engagement_events` - opens, clicks, etc.
- `sequence_branch_rules` - conditional logic

---

### 7. **GMAIL CONNECTOR DEFINITION** (`internal/connector/definitions/gmail.yaml`)

```yaml
id: gmail
name: Gmail
type: email
tier: 1 (required)
schema_version: 1

auth:
  method: oauth2
  authorize_url: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth
  token_url: https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
  scopes:
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send

verify:
  url: "https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/profile"
  method: GET
  account_fields: [emailAddress]
```

**Capabilities:**
- Read sent mail (gmail.readonly)
- Send emails (gmail.send)

---

### 8. **DATABASE SCHEMA** (`internal/db/schema.go`)

**Core Tables:**

| Table | Purpose | Key Fields |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| `connectors` | OAuth connections | id, type, connected_at, sync_cursor, last_sync_at, total_synced |
| `contacts` | Prospects | id, source, email, domain, company, title, lead_status, tier, tier_score, email_count |
| `emails` | Gmail messages | id, source, direction, thread_id, subject, from_email, to_emails, date, class |
| `knowledge_atoms` | Assembled context | id, atom_type, scope, content (JSON), source, confidence, use_count |
| `research_dossiers` | Per-prospect briefs | id, contact_email, company_domain, dossier (text), tier, research_depth |
| `learnings` | Captured insights | id, type, content, source_session, created_at |
| `skill_versions` | Skill history | id, skill_name, version, content, email_count, learning_count, active |
| `strategic_memory` | Distilled insights | id, category, summary, affected_skills (JSON), evidence, source_key, priority |
| `sessions` | Session tracking | id, model, source, status, started_at, ended_at |
| `session_events` | Session activity | id, session_id, type, payload (JSON) |
| `sequence_enrollments` | Active sequences | id, sequence_name, contact_email, mailbox_id, status, current_step, next_due_at |
| `follow_up_tasks` | Scheduled sends | id, enrollment_id, contact_email, step_number, send_type, status, due_at |
| `mailboxes` | Email accounts | id, email, provider, first_touch_daily_cap, follow_up_daily_cap, warmup_stage, status |
| `mailbox_daily_usage` | Daily caps | mailbox_id, usage_day, first_touches_sent, follow_ups_sent |
| `mailbox_send_events` | Immutable send log | id, mailbox_id, send_type, sent_at, task_id |
| `send_attempts` | Send history | id, mailbox_id, task_id, contact_email, status, error |
| `suppression` | Blocklists | id, scope (email|domain), value, reason, source |
| `reply_events` | Inbound replies | id, enrollment_id, contact_email, classification, status, occurred_at |

---

### 9. **PIPELINE COMMANDS** (`cmd/pipeline/pipeline.go`)

**Command:** `nurge pipeline status [--format json]`

```go
type statusCmd struct {
    Format string // "json" or text
}

func runStatus(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // Fetches PipelineStatus from db
    // Outputs JSON or text dashboard
}
```

**Returns:** Full PipelineStatus struct (JSON/formatted text)

---

### 10. **TIER SCORING SYSTEM** (`internal/tier/`)

**Scoring Dimensions:**

```go
type TierScore struct {
    ICPFit        float64  // 0-100: industry, company size, funding, geography, title
    BuyingTrigger float64  // 0-100: funding, job change, hiring, product launch, expansion
    IntentSignal  float64  // 0-100: prior emails, reply history, active sequence
    Accessibility float64  // 0-100: has direct email, phone, LinkedIn, website
    Total         float64  // weighted sum
    Tier          int      // 1 (70+), 2 (40-69), 3 (<40), 0 (unscored)
    Reasons       []string // explanation text
}

// Weights:
const (
    weightICPFit        = 0.40
    weightBuyingTrigger = 0.25
    weightIntentSignal  = 0.20
    weightAccessibility = 0.15
)
```

**Scoring Function:**
```go
func ScoreContact(contact ContactData, icp ICPConfig, hasReply bool, hasActiveSequence bool) TierScore
```

**ICP Config:**
```go
type ICPConfig struct {
    TargetIndustries []string // e.g., ["SaaS", "Fintech"]
    CompanySizeRange string   // e.g., "50-500"
    FundingStage     string   // e.g., "series-a"
    Geography        string   // e.g., "global", "US", "EU"
    TargetTitles     []string // e.g., ["VP Sales", "CRO"]
}
```

---

### 11. **ASSEMBLY SYSTEM** (`internal/assembly/types.go`)

**Task Types (15 types):**
```go
const (
    TaskOutbound   = "outbound"
    TaskResearch   = "research"
    TaskQualify    = "qualify"
    TaskFollowup   = "followup"
    TaskReply      = "reply"
    TaskPipeline   = "pipeline"
    TaskSequence   = "sequence"
    TaskAnalytics  = "analytics"
    TaskProspects  = "prospects"
    TaskProduct    = "product"
    TaskICP        = "icp"
    TaskPersonas   = "personas"
    TaskBook       = "book"
    TaskHygiene    = "hygiene"
    TaskSendHealth = "sending_health"
    TaskReview     = "review"
)
```

**Task → Atom Mapping:**
```go
var taskAtomMap = map[TaskType][]AtomType{
    TaskOutbound: {AtomTone, AtomHooks, AtomPositioning, AtomObjections, AtomCompliance},
    TaskFollowup: {AtomTone, AtomHooks, AtomPositioning, AtomObjections, AtomSignals},
    TaskReply:    {AtomTone, AtomObjections, AtomPositioning},
    TaskResearch: {AtomPersonas, AtomPositioning, AtomSignals},
    // ... more
}
```

**Assemble Request/Result:**
```go
type AssembleRequest struct {
    Task     TaskType
    Prospect string // email address
    Format   string // markdown, json, text
}

type AssembleResult struct {
    Context       string   // assembled markdown/text/json
    TokenEstimate int      // estimated tokens
    AtomsLoaded   []string // atom IDs used
    Directives    []string // behavioral instructions
    Cached        bool     // from 5-min cache?
}
```

---

### Key Integration Points for New Code:

1. **Must update `distilled_at` column** to learnings table schema
2. **Distillation appends to "learnings" JSON array** inside atoms, preserving original content
3. **Pipeline status** feeds into banner and hooks — changes here affect all CLI output
4. **Sequence operations** are transactional with draft generation — maintain atomicity
5. **Tier scoring** is deterministic, config-driven; use `LoadICPConfig()` to read settings
6. **Assembly** maps tasks → atoms dynamically; affects LLM context quality
7. **Contacts table** is the source of truth; enrichment updates here (`tier`, `company`, `title`, etc.)
8. **Session hooks** run on every prompt — keep them fast and graceful
9. **Knowledge atoms** use `Scope` field for persona/segment targeting — leverage for personalization
10. **Gmail sync** maintains a cursor per connector — idempotent design, never re-fetch

Let me know which specific areas you want to dive deeper into!

   - Stale skills
   - Pipeline status
6. Outputs JSON with:
   - `SystemMessage` (visible banner with pipeline dashboard)
   - `AdditionalContext` (LLM prompt context)

**Banner includes:**
- Version info
- Pipeline dashboard (new outreach, follow-ups, funnel, sources)
- Nudge message if not healthy
- Sync status
- Stale skills refresh suggestions

**Prompt Context includes:**
- User context (name, role)
- Setup status
- Pipeline state
- Source effectiveness
- Per-persona pipeline
- Sync context
- Strategic memory + stale skills
- KPI context
- Sending context
- Geography/compliance notes
- Available skills

---

### 5. **EMAIL SYNC SYSTEM** (`internal/email/sync.go`)

**What it stores:**

```go
type RawMessage struct {
    ID          string // Gmail message ID
    Source      string // "gmail" or other
    Direction   string // "outbound" or "inbound"
    ThreadID    string
    Subject     string
    FromEmail   string
    ToEmails    string // JSON array
    CcEmails    string // JSON array
    Date        string // RFC3339
    Snippet     string
    BodyPreview string
    Labels      string // JSON array of label IDs
}

type SyncResult struct {
    Fetched  int           // messages seen
    Stored   int           // messages inserted
    Cursor   string        // sync cursor (RFC3339 timestamp)
    Duration time.Duration
}
```

**Tables:**
- `emails` - raw message storage
- `contacts` - created/updated from To/Cc/From addresses

**Sync Logic:**
- First run: fetches last 90 days of sent mail (`in:sent` query)
- Subsequent: uses cursor to fetch only new
- Upserts contacts for each recipient/sender
- Updates `connectors` table with cursor + total_synced

**Function Signatures:**
```go
func Sync(srv *gm.Service, d *sql.DB) (*SyncResult, error)
func StoreEmail(d *sql.DB, e *RawMessage) error
func StoreConvexEmails(d *sql.DB, emails []RawMessage) (*SyncResult, error)
```

---

### 6. **SEQUENCE SYSTEM** (`internal/sequence/`)

**Key Types:**

```go
type Enrollment struct {
    ID            string // seq:xxx
    SequenceName  string
    CampaignName  string
    MailboxID     string
    MailboxEmail  string
    ContactEmail  string
    Status        string // active|paused|completed|stopped
    CurrentStep   int    // 1-indexed
    TotalSteps    int
    LastSentAt    string
    LastReplyAt   string
    NextDueAt     string
    ThreadID      string
    LastMessageID string
    InitialBody   string
    ReplyState    string
}

type Task struct {
    ID            string // task:xxx
    EnrollmentID  string
    MailboxID     string
    ContactEmail  string
    StepNumber    int
    StepName      string
    SendType      string // first_touch|follow_up|reply|manual
    Status        string // open|completed|canceled
    SendStatus    string // pending|sent
    DueAt         string
    Body          string
}

type Template struct {
    Name  string         // sequence name
    Steps []TemplateStep
}

type TemplateStep struct {
    StepNumber      int
    Name            string
    SendType        string
    Delay           string // "72h", "1m", "0s"
    SubjectTemplate string
    BodyTemplate    string
}
```

**Status Constants:**
```go
const (
    StatusActive    = "active"
    StatusPaused    = "paused"
    StatusCompleted = "completed"
    StatusStopped   = "stopped"
    
    TaskStatusOpen      = "open"
    TaskStatusCompleted = "completed"
    TaskStatusCanceled  = "canceled"
    
    SendTypeFirstTouch = "first_touch"
    SendTypeFollowUp   = "follow_up"
    SendTypeReply      = "reply"
    SendTypeManual     = "manual"
)
```

**Built-in Templates:**
- `default-3-touch` (1st touch, 72h follow-up, 96h follow-up)
- `gentle-2-touch` (1st touch, 120h follow-up)
- `test-3-touch-1m` (for testing)

**Core Operations:**
```go
func CreateEnrollmentAfterFirstTouch(d *sql.DB, opts EnrollmentOptions) (*Enrollment, *Task, error)
func AdvanceTaskAfterSend(d *sql.DB, opts AdvanceOptions) (*Task, error)
func EnrollmentByID(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) (*Enrollment, error)
func ListEnrollments(d *sql.DB, status string, limit int) ([]Enrollment, error)
func ActiveEnrollmentForContact(d *sql.DB, mailboxID, contactEmail string) (*Enrollment, error)
func PauseEnrollment(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) error
func ResumeEnrollment(d *sql.DB, enrollmentID string) error
```

**Tables:**
- `sequence_enrollments` - active sequences per contact
- `follow_up_tasks` - scheduled follow-ups
- `sequence_templates` - template definitions
- `sequence_drafts` - pre-generated follow-up content
- `campaigns` - campaign metadata
- `suppressions` - domain/email blocklists
- `reply_events` - inbound replies tracked by enrollment
- `engagement_events` - opens, clicks, etc.
- `sequence_branch_rules` - conditional logic

---

### 7. **GMAIL CONNECTOR DEFINITION** (`internal/connector/definitions/gmail.yaml`)

```yaml
id: gmail
name: Gmail
type: email
tier: 1 (required)
schema_version: 1

auth:
  method: oauth2
  authorize_url: https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth
  token_url: https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token
  scopes:
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.readonly
    - https://www.googleapis.com/auth/gmail.send

verify:
  url: "https://gmail.googleapis.com/gmail/v1/users/me/profile"
  method: GET
  account_fields: [emailAddress]
```

**Capabilities:**
- Read sent mail (gmail.readonly)
- Send emails (gmail.send)

---

### 8. **DATABASE SCHEMA** (`internal/db/schema.go`)

**Core Tables:**

| Table | Purpose | Key Fields |
|-------|---------|-----------|
| `connectors` | OAuth connections | id, type, connected_at, sync_cursor, last_sync_at, total_synced |
| `contacts` | Prospects | id, source, email, domain, company, title, lead_status, tier, tier_score, email_count |
| `emails` | Gmail messages | id, source, direction, thread_id, subject, from_email, to_emails, date, class |
| `knowledge_atoms` | Assembled context | id, atom_type, scope, content (JSON), source, confidence, use_count |
| `research_dossiers` | Per-prospect briefs | id, contact_email, company_domain, dossier (text), tier, research_depth |
| `learnings` | Captured insights | id, type, content, source_session, created_at |
| `skill_versions` | Skill history | id, skill_name, version, content, email_count, learning_count, active |
| `strategic_memory` | Distilled insights | id, category, summary, affected_skills (JSON), evidence, source_key, priority |
| `sessions` | Session tracking | id, model, source, status, started_at, ended_at |
| `session_events` | Session activity | id, session_id, type, payload (JSON) |
| `sequence_enrollments` | Active sequences | id, sequence_name, contact_email, mailbox_id, status, current_step, next_due_at |
| `follow_up_tasks` | Scheduled sends | id, enrollment_id, contact_email, step_number, send_type, status, due_at |
| `mailboxes` | Email accounts | id, email, provider, first_touch_daily_cap, follow_up_daily_cap, warmup_stage, status |
| `mailbox_daily_usage` | Daily caps | mailbox_id, usage_day, first_touches_sent, follow_ups_sent |
| `mailbox_send_events` | Immutable send log | id, mailbox_id, send_type, sent_at, task_id |
| `send_attempts` | Send history | id, mailbox_id, task_id, contact_email, status, error |
| `suppression` | Blocklists | id, scope (email|domain), value, reason, source |
| `reply_events` | Inbound replies | id, enrollment_id, contact_email, classification, status, occurred_at |

---

### 9. **PIPELINE COMMANDS** (`cmd/pipeline/pipeline.go`)

**Command:** `nurge pipeline status [--format json]`

```go
type statusCmd struct {
    Format string // "json" or text
}

func runStatus(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    // Fetches PipelineStatus from db
    // Outputs JSON or text dashboard
}
```

**Returns:** Full PipelineStatus struct (JSON/formatted text)

---

### 10. **TIER SCORING SYSTEM** (`internal/tier/`)

**Scoring Dimensions:**

```go
type TierScore struct {
    ICPFit        float64  // 0-100: industry, company size, funding, geography, title
    BuyingTrigger float64  // 0-100: funding, job change, hiring, product launch, expansion
    IntentSignal  float64  // 0-100: prior emails, reply history, active sequence
    Accessibility float64  // 0-100: has direct email, phone, LinkedIn, website
    Total         float64  // weighted sum
    Tier          int      // 1 (70+), 2 (40-69), 3 (<40), 0 (unscored)
    Reasons       []string // explanation text
}

// Weights:
const (
    weightICPFit        = 0.40
    weightBuyingTrigger = 0.25
    weightIntentSignal  = 0.20
    weightAccessibility = 0.15
)
```

**Scoring Function:**
```go
func ScoreContact(contact ContactData, icp ICPConfig, hasReply bool, hasActiveSequence bool) TierScore
```

**ICP Config:**
```go
type ICPConfig struct {
    TargetIndustries []string // e.g., ["SaaS", "Fintech"]
    CompanySizeRange string   // e.g., "50-500"
    FundingStage     string   // e.g., "series-a"
    Geography        string   // e.g., "global", "US", "EU"
    TargetTitles     []string // e.g., ["VP Sales", "CRO"]
}
```

---

### 11. **ASSEMBLY SYSTEM** (`internal/assembly/types.go`)

**Task Types (15 types):**
```go
const (
    TaskOutbound   = "outbound"
    TaskResearch   = "research"
    TaskQualify    = "qualify"
    TaskFollowup   = "followup"
    TaskReply      = "reply"
    TaskPipeline   = "pipeline"
    TaskSequence   = "sequence"
    TaskAnalytics  = "analytics"
    TaskProspects  = "prospects"
    TaskProduct    = "product"
    TaskICP        = "icp"
    TaskPersonas   = "personas"
    TaskBook       = "book"
    TaskHygiene    = "hygiene"
    TaskSendHealth = "sending_health"
    TaskReview     = "review"
)
```

**Task → Atom Mapping:**
```go
var taskAtomMap = map[TaskType][]AtomType{
    TaskOutbound: {AtomTone, AtomHooks, AtomPositioning, AtomObjections, AtomCompliance},
    TaskFollowup: {AtomTone, AtomHooks, AtomPositioning, AtomObjections, AtomSignals},
    TaskReply:    {AtomTone, AtomObjections, AtomPositioning},
    TaskResearch: {AtomPersonas, AtomPositioning, AtomSignals},
    // ... more
}
```

**Assemble Request/Result:**
```go
type AssembleRequest struct {
    Task     TaskType
    Prospect string // email address
    Format   string // markdown, json, text
}

type AssembleResult struct {
    Context       string   // assembled markdown/text/json
    TokenEstimate int      // estimated tokens
    AtomsLoaded   []string // atom IDs used
    Directives    []string // behavioral instructions
    Cached        bool     // from 5-min cache?
}
```

---

### Key Integration Points for New Code:

1. **Must update `distilled_at` column** to learnings table schema
2. **Distillation appends to "learnings" JSON array** inside atoms, preserving original content
3. **Pipeline status** feeds into banner and hooks — changes here affect all CLI output
4. **Sequence operations** are transactional with draft generation — maintain atomicity
5. **Tier scoring** is deterministic, config-driven; use `LoadICPConfig()` to read settings
6. **Assembly** maps tasks → atoms dynamically; affects LLM context quality
7. **Contacts table** is the source of truth; enrichment updates here (`tier`, `company`, `title`, etc.)
8. **Session hooks** run on every prompt — keep them fast and graceful
9. **Knowledge atoms** use `Scope` field for persona/segment targeting — leverage for personalization
10. **Gmail sync** maintains a cursor per connector — idempotent design, never re-fetch

Let me know which specific areas you want to dive deeper into!

content.text:
Good, I have a clear picture of the codebase. Here's my first question:

The biggest gap is the **Signals phase** (0% built). Process.md describes 5 signal categories: career transitions, financial/strategic, org growth, digital behavior, and competitive displacement. But most of these require paid APIs (UserGems, Crunchbase, LinkedIn) or scraping infrastructure.

Given you're bootstrapped with near-zero enrichment budget, **where should signals come from?**

A) **User-reported only** - user tells Claude "John just changed jobs" or "Acme raised a round", Claude records the signal and adjusts priority. Zero cost, fully manual.

B) **Free scraping + user-reported** - `nurge scrape` fetches LinkedIn profiles, company websites, news via free sources. Claude analyzes for signals. Supplement with user-reported. Near-zero cost, partially automated.

C) **Stub the full system, wire up later** - build the signal storage/scoring/decay infrastructure in Go, but leave the actual detection to future API integrations. Skills can reference signals when they exist. Zero cost now, ready for APIs later.

Which approach fits where you are right now?

content.content: docs/signals.md
docs/research-outbound-frameworks.md
docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-26-enrichment-context-assembly-design.md

1→# Outbound Signals Reference
     2→
     3→Signals are data points that tell you whether a prospect is worth reaching out to, when, and how. Nurge's research and qualification skills use these signals to build prospect dossiers and score leads.
     4→
     5→**Key principle:** Most signals are findable via web search and native Claude tools (WebSearch, WebFetch). No paid APIs required for the core signal set. Enrichment connectors (ContactOut, Apollo, Clay) add structured data on top.
     6→
     7→**Parallel execution:** When researching a prospect, Claude should dispatch multiple agents in parallel — one per signal category. Each agent runs its own web searches independently and reports back. This turns a 5-minute serial research task into a 30-second parallel one.
     8→
     9→---
    10→
    11→## Signal Categories
    12→
    13→### 1. Buying Signals (They Might Need What You Sell)
    14→
    15→| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
    16→|--------|--------------|------------------|
    17→| **Recently raised funding** | New budget, growth mandate, likely hiring and buying tools | WebSearch: "[company] funding round 2025 2026", Crunchbase, TechCrunch, company blog |
    18→| **Hiring for relevant roles** | Building a team = buying tools for that team | WebSearch: "[company] hiring [role]", company careers page (WebFetch /careers), LinkedIn jobs, Greenhouse/Lever pages |
    19→| **Job posts mention your problem space** | Explicit signal they have the pain you solve | WebSearch: "[company] jobs [keyword from your product]", Indeed, LinkedIn |
    20→| **Tech stack changes** | Migrating or adopting new tech = buying window | WebSearch: "[company] migrates to [tech]", BuiltWith, StackShare, GitHub repos |
    21→| **Headcount growth** | Growing company = growing needs | WebSearch: "[company] headcount growth", LinkedIn company page (via search) |
    22→| **New leadership** | New CTO/VP often brings new tools, new strategy | WebSearch: "[company] new CTO/VP/Head of", LinkedIn (via search) |
    23→| **Expanding to new markets** | New geo/vertical = new infrastructure needs | WebSearch: "[company] expansion", press releases, company blog |
    24→| **Product launch** | Shipping something new = needs around that launch | WebSearch: "[company] launches", Product Hunt, company blog |
    25→| **Partnerships announced** | Signals growth and strategic direction | WebSearch: "[company] partnership", press releases |
    26→| **Pain signals in reviews** | Users complaining about problems you solve | WebSearch: "site:g2.com [company]", "site:glassdoor.com [company]", Capterra reviews |
    27→| **Competitor churn** | They're leaving a competitor = active buyer | WebSearch: "[company] switches from [competitor]", G2 reviews mentioning competitors |
    28→| **RFP/procurement signals** | Actively buying | WebSearch: "[company] RFP [category]", government procurement sites |
    29→
    30→### 2. Timing Signals (Right Moment to Reach Out)
    31→
    32→| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
    33→|--------|--------------|------------------|
    34→| **Fiscal year start** | New budget cycle = spending authority | WebSearch: "[company] fiscal year", SEC filings for public companies |
    35→| **End of quarter** | Budget pressure to spend remaining allocation | Calendar-based (Q1: Jan-Mar, Q2: Apr-Jun, etc.) |
    36→| **Contract renewal period** | Evaluating alternatives | Hard to find externally — infer from when they adopted current tool |
    37→| **Competitor incident** | Their current vendor failed them | WebSearch: "[competitor] outage/breach/issue", Twitter/X, Hacker News |
    38→| **Industry regulation change** | Compliance deadline = forced buying | WebSearch: "[industry] regulation 2026", industry publications |
    39→| **Conference/event season** | Decision-makers are thinking about the space | WebSearch: "[industry] conference 2026", event websites |
    40→| **Recent leadership change** | New leader = 90-day window to make changes | WebSearch: "[company] appoints [role]", LinkedIn |
    41→| **Recent layoffs** | May be looking to do more with less (efficiency tools) | WebSearch: "[company] layoffs", LinkedIn |
    42→
    43→### 3. Fit Signals (They Match Your ICP)
    44→
    45→| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
    46→|--------|--------------|------------------|
    47→| **Company size** | Within your ICP range | WebSearch: "[company] employees", LinkedIn company page, Crunchbase |
    48→| **Industry/vertical** | Matches your target market | Company website (WebFetch), LinkedIn, Crunchbase |
    49→| **Revenue range** | Can afford your product | WebSearch: "[company] revenue", Crunchbase, SEC filings, Inc 5000, Deloitte Fast 500 |
    50→| **Growth stage** | Startup/growth/enterprise matches your sweet spot | Crunchbase (funding rounds), LinkedIn headcount, press coverage volume |
    51→| **Geography** | Within your target regions | Company website, LinkedIn, WebSearch |
    52→| **Technology used** | Uses tech your product integrates with or replaces | BuiltWith (WebFetch), StackShare, GitHub, job postings (tech requirements) |
    53→| **Business model** | B2B/B2C/SaaS/marketplace — matches your target | Company website (WebFetch) |
    54→| **Customer profile** | Who THEY sell to tells you about their sophistication | Company website, case studies, G2 profile |
    55→
    56→### 4. Persona Signals (About the Individual)
    57→
    58→| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
    59→|--------|--------------|------------------|
    60→| **Recent job change** | New role = looking to make impact, open to new tools | WebSearch: "[person] joins [company]", LinkedIn (via search) |
    61→| **Published content** | Know what they think about, how they communicate | WebSearch: "[person] blog/article/podcast", LinkedIn posts (via search), Medium, Substack |
    62→| **Conference speaker** | Thought leader, influences decisions | WebSearch: "[person] speaker [conference]", YouTube, event sites |
    63→| **Open source contributor** | Technical, values transparency | GitHub profile, WebSearch: "[person] github" |
    64→| **Social media activity** | What they share = what they care about | WebSearch: "[person] twitter/linkedin", LinkedIn posts (via search) |
    65→| **Book/course author** | Deep expertise in specific area, known authority | WebSearch: "[person] book/course", Amazon |
    66→| **Board/advisory roles** | Influential, connected | WebSearch: "[person] advisor/board", LinkedIn (via search) |
    67→| **Education background** | Shared connections, communication style | LinkedIn (via search), university alumni pages |
    68→| **Mutual connections** | Warm intro potential | Hard via web search — better with LinkedIn Sales Nav (future connector) |
    69→
    70→### 5. Risk Signals (Don't Reach Out / Proceed with Caution)
    71→
    72→| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
    73→|--------|--------------|------------------|
    74→| **Competitor** | They sell the same thing you do | WebSearch: "[company] vs [your product]", company website (WebFetch), G2 category |
    75→| **Already a customer** | Don't cold email your own customers | `nurge emails list --format json`, CRM data if connected |
    76→| **Previously rejected** | Respect the no — or wait significant time | `nurge emails list --format json` (check for negative replies) |
    77→| **Unsubscribed/bounced** | Legal obligation to not contact | Suppression list in nurge-hygiene |
    78→| **Company in trouble** | Layoffs, bankruptcy, acquisition = not buying | WebSearch: "[company] layoffs/bankruptcy/acquired", news |
    79→| **Bad domain reputation** | Their emails might not reach you either | DNS check, but usually not relevant for outbound |
    80→| **Recently contacted by team** | Don't double-tap from multiple people | `nurge emails list --format json`, CRM data |
    81→| **Litigation/controversy** | Risky to associate with | WebSearch: "[company] lawsuit/controversy" |
    82→
    83→---
    84→
    85→## Parallel Research Architecture
    86→
    87→When researching a prospect, Claude should dispatch **parallel agents** — one per signal category. This is the key to fast, thorough research.
    88→
    89→### Agent Dispatch Pattern
    90→
    91→```
    92→User: "Research Acme Corp / Jane Smith"
    93→         │
    94→         ├─→ Agent 1: COMPANY SIGNALS
    95→         │     WebSearch: "[company] funding"
    96→         │     WebSearch: "[company] hiring"
    97→         │     WebSearch: "[company] news 2026"
    98→         │     WebFetch: company website
    99→         │     WebFetch: company careers page
   100→         │
   101→         ├─→ Agent 2: PERSON SIGNALS
   102→         │     WebSearch: "[person] [company]"
   103→         │     WebSearch: "[person] speaker conference"
   104→         │     WebSearch: "[person] blog article"
   105→         │     WebSearch: "site:linkedin.com [person] posts"
   106→         │
   107→         ├─→ Agent 3: FIT + RISK CHECK
   108→         │     Compare company against ICP (from config)
   109→         │     Check competitor match (from config)
   110→         │     Check prior emails (nurge emails list)
   111→         │     WebSearch: "[company] vs [our product]"
   112→         │
   113→         ├─→ Agent 4: CONTACT INFO
   114→         │     WebSearch: "[person] email [company]"
   115→         │     WebFetch: company /contact or /mentions-legales or /impressum
   116→         │     WebSearch: "site:contactout.com [person]"
   117→         │     WebSearch: "site:rocketreach.co [person]"
   118→         │
   119→         └─→ Agent 5: TIMING SIGNALS
   120→               WebSearch: "[company] Q1 2026 plans"
   121→               WebSearch: "[competitor of their current tool] issues"
   122→               Check fiscal year / industry events
   123→```
   124→
   125→### All agents return → Combine into Dossier
   126→
   127→```
   128→Prospect Dossier: Jane Smith, CTO @ Acme Corp
   129→──────────────────────────────────────────────
   130→FIT:      Strong match (Series B, 120 employees, SaaS, US)
   131→RISK:     None detected (not a competitor, no prior contact)
   132→BUYING:   Just raised $15M Series B (2 months ago), hiring 3 engineers
   133→TIMING:   New CTO (started 4 months ago), fiscal year starts Jan
   134→PERSONA:  Spoke at DevConf 2025 on "scaling engineering teams",
   135→          posts about developer productivity, values transparency
   136→CONTACT:  jane@acme.com (from careers page), LinkedIn: /in/janesmith
   137→ANGLE:    Reference her DevConf talk on scaling → your product helps
   138→          engineering teams ship faster → relevant to her $15M growth mandate
   139→
   140→CONFIDENCE: High (multiple corroborating sources)
   141→```
   142→
   143→### Present to user for review before drafting.
   144→
   145→---
   146→
   147→## Signal Sources Summary
   148→
   149→| Source | Free? | What It Gets You | How to Access |
   150→|--------|-------|-----------------|---------------|
   151→| **Google (WebSearch)** | Yes | Everything — the starting point for all signals | WebSearch tool |
   152→| **Company website** | Yes | Product info, team, careers, legal pages (contact info) | WebFetch tool |
   153→| **LinkedIn posts** | Yes | What prospects think about, recent activity | WebSearch: "site:linkedin.com [person]" |
   154→| **LinkedIn profile** | Blocked | Direct scrape fails (403/999) | Use web search to get profile snippets |
   155→| **Crunchbase** | Partial | Funding, investors, company info | WebSearch (basic), WebFetch (some pages) |
   156→| **G2** | Yes | Reviews, competitor comparisons, pain signals | WebSearch: "site:g2.com [company]" |
   157→| **Glassdoor** | Yes | Company culture, leadership sentiment | WebSearch: "site:glassdoor.com [company]" |
   158→| **GitHub** | Yes | Tech stack, open source activity, contributor profiles | WebSearch/WebFetch |
   159→| **Product Hunt** | Yes | Product launches, tech adoption | WebSearch: "site:producthunt.com [company]" |
   160→| **Twitter/X** | Partial | Real-time signals, opinions, announcements | WebSearch: "site:twitter.com [person/company]" |
   161→| **News (TechCrunch, etc.)** | Yes | Funding, launches, leadership changes | WebSearch |
   162→| **Job boards** | Yes | Hiring signals, tech stack from requirements | WebSearch, WebFetch careers pages |
   163→| **SEC/EDGAR** | Yes | Revenue, financials (public companies only) | WebSearch, WebFetch |
   164→| **Mentions Légales** | Yes | Contact info (French companies, legally required) | WebFetch: [company].fr/mentionslegales |
   165→| **Impressum** | Yes | Contact info (German companies, legally required) | WebFetch: [company].de/impressum |
   166→| **ContactOut** | Paid | Verified email + phone from LinkedIn URL | Future connector |
   167→| **Apollo** | Freemium | Email, company data, technographics | Future connector |
   168→| **Clay** | Paid | Waterfall enrichment from multiple sources | Future connector |
   169→| **BuiltWith** | Freemium | Technology stack detection | WebFetch (basic), future connector |
   170→
   171→---
   172→
   173→## How Skills Use Signals
   174→
   175→| Skill | Which Signals | How |
   176→|-------|--------------|-----|
   177→| **nurge-research** | All of them — builds the full dossier | Dispatches parallel agents per category |
   178→| **nurge-qualify** | Fit + Risk + Buying | Scores against ICP, checks competitor, checks BANT |
   179→| **nurge-outbound** | Persona + Timing + Buying | Uses signals to personalize the email angle |
   180→| **nurge-sequence** | Timing | Determines sequence cadence and urgency |
   181→| **nurge-followup** | Buying + Timing | References trigger events in follow-ups |
   182→| **nurge-review** | All (aggregated) | Surfaces which signal types correlate with replies |
   183→

file.content:
# Outbound Signals Reference

Signals are data points that tell you whether a prospect is worth reaching out to, when, and how. Nurge's research and qualification skills use these signals to build prospect dossiers and score leads.

**Key principle:** Most signals are findable via web search and native Claude tools (WebSearch, WebFetch). No paid APIs required for the core signal set. Enrichment connectors (ContactOut, Apollo, Clay) add structured data on top.

**Parallel execution:** When researching a prospect, Claude should dispatch multiple agents in parallel — one per signal category. Each agent runs its own web searches independently and reports back. This turns a 5-minute serial research task into a 30-second parallel one.

---

## Signal Categories

### 1. Buying Signals (They Might Need What You Sell)

| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
|--------|--------------|------------------|
| **Recently raised funding** | New budget, growth mandate, likely hiring and buying tools | WebSearch: "[company] funding round 2025 2026", Crunchbase, TechCrunch, company blog |
| **Hiring for relevant roles** | Building a team = buying tools for that team | WebSearch: "[company] hiring [role]", company careers page (WebFetch /careers), LinkedIn jobs, Greenhouse/Lever pages |
| **Job posts mention your problem space** | Explicit signal they have the pain you solve | WebSearch: "[company] jobs [keyword from your product]", Indeed, LinkedIn |
| **Tech stack changes** | Migrating or adopting new tech = buying window | WebSearch: "[company] migrates to [tech]", BuiltWith, StackShare, GitHub repos |
| **Headcount growth** | Growing company = growing needs | WebSearch: "[company] headcount growth", LinkedIn company page (via search) |
| **New leadership** | New CTO/VP often brings new tools, new strategy | WebSearch: "[company] new CTO/VP/Head of", LinkedIn (via search) |
| **Expanding to new markets** | New geo/vertical = new infrastructure needs | WebSearch: "[company] expansion", press releases, company blog |
| **Product launch** | Shipping something new = needs around that launch | WebSearch: "[company] launches", Product Hunt, company blog |
| **Partnerships announced** | Signals growth and strategic direction | WebSearch: "[company] partnership", press releases |
| **Pain signals in reviews** | Users complaining about problems you solve | WebSearch: "site:g2.com [company]", "site:glassdoor.com [company]", Capterra reviews |
| **Competitor churn** | They're leaving a competitor = active buyer | WebSearch: "[company] switches from [competitor]", G2 reviews mentioning competitors |
| **RFP/procurement signals** | Actively buying | WebSearch: "[company] RFP [category]", government procurement sites |

### 2. Timing Signals (Right Moment to Reach Out)

| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
|--------|--------------|------------------|
| **Fiscal year start** | New budget cycle = spending authority | WebSearch: "[company] fiscal year", SEC filings for public companies |
| **End of quarter** | Budget pressure to spend remaining allocation | Calendar-based (Q1: Jan-Mar, Q2: Apr-Jun, etc.) |
| **Contract renewal period** | Evaluating alternatives | Hard to find externally — infer from when they adopted current tool |
| **Competitor incident** | Their current vendor failed them | WebSearch: "[competitor] outage/breach/issue", Twitter/X, Hacker News |
| **Industry regulation change** | Compliance deadline = forced buying | WebSearch: "[industry] regulation 2026", industry publications |
| **Conference/event season** | Decision-makers are thinking about the space | WebSearch: "[industry] conference 2026", event websites |
| **Recent leadership change** | New leader = 90-day window to make changes | WebSearch: "[company] appoints [role]", LinkedIn |
| **Recent layoffs** | May be looking to do more with less (efficiency tools) | WebSearch: "[company] layoffs", LinkedIn |

### 3. Fit Signals (They Match Your ICP)

| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
|--------|--------------|------------------|
| **Company size** | Within your ICP range | WebSearch: "[company] employees", LinkedIn company page, Crunchbase |
| **Industry/vertical** | Matches your target market | Company website (WebFetch), LinkedIn, Crunchbase |
| **Revenue range** | Can afford your product | WebSearch: "[company] revenue", Crunchbase, SEC filings, Inc 5000, Deloitte Fast 500 |
| **Growth stage** | Startup/growth/enterprise matches your sweet spot | Crunchbase (funding rounds), LinkedIn headcount, press coverage volume |
| **Geography** | Within your target regions | Company website, LinkedIn, WebSearch |
| **Technology used** | Uses tech your product integrates with or replaces | BuiltWith (WebFetch), StackShare, GitHub, job postings (tech requirements) |
| **Business model** | B2B/B2C/SaaS/marketplace — matches your target | Company website (WebFetch) |
| **Customer profile** | Who THEY sell to tells you about their sophistication | Company website, case studies, G2 profile |

### 4. Persona Signals (About the Individual)

| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
|--------|--------------|------------------|
| **Recent job change** | New role = looking to make impact, open to new tools | WebSearch: "[person] joins [company]", LinkedIn (via search) |
| **Published content** | Know what they think about, how they communicate | WebSearch: "[person] blog/article/podcast", LinkedIn posts (via search), Medium, Substack |
| **Conference speaker** | Thought leader, influences decisions | WebSearch: "[person] speaker [conference]", YouTube, event sites |
| **Open source contributor** | Technical, values transparency | GitHub profile, WebSearch: "[person] github" |
| **Social media activity** | What they share = what they care about | WebSearch: "[person] twitter/linkedin", LinkedIn posts (via search) |
| **Book/course author** | Deep expertise in specific area, known authority | WebSearch: "[person] book/course", Amazon |
| **Board/advisory roles** | Influential, connected | WebSearch: "[person] advisor/board", LinkedIn (via search) |
| **Education background** | Shared connections, communication style | LinkedIn (via search), university alumni pages |
| **Mutual connections** | Warm intro potential | Hard via web search — better with LinkedIn Sales Nav (future connector) |

### 5. Risk Signals (Don't Reach Out / Proceed with Caution)

| Signal | What It Means | Where to Find It |
|--------|--------------|------------------|
| **Competitor** | They sell the same thing you do | WebSearch: "[company] vs [your product]", company website (WebFetch), G2 category |
| **Already a customer** | Don't cold email your own customers | `nurge emails list --format json`, CRM data if connected |
| **Previously rejected** | Respect the no — or wait significant time | `nurge emails list --format json` (check for negative replies) |
| **Unsubscribed/bounced** | Legal obligation to not contact | Suppression list in nurge-hygiene |
| **Company in trouble** | Layoffs, bankruptcy, acquisition = not buying | WebSearch: "[company] layoffs/bankruptcy/acquired", news |
| **Bad domain reputation** | Their emails might not reach you either | DNS check, but usually not relevant for outbound |
| **Recently contacted by team** | Don't double-tap from multiple people | `nurge emails list --format json`, CRM data |
| **Litigation/controversy** | Risky to associate with | WebSearch: "[company] lawsuit/controversy" |

---

## Parallel Research Architecture

When researching a prospect, Claude should dispatch **parallel agents** — one per signal category. This is the key to fast, thorough research.

### Agent Dispatch Pattern

```
User: "Research Acme Corp / Jane Smith"
         │
         ├─→ Agent 1: COMPANY SIGNALS
         │     WebSearch: "[company] funding"
         │     WebSearch: "[company] hiring"
         │     WebSearch: "[company] news 2026"
         │     WebFetch: company website
         │     WebFetch: company careers page
         │
         ├─→ Agent 2: PERSON SIGNALS
         │     WebSearch: "[person] [company]"
         │     WebSearch: "[person] speaker conference"
         │     WebSearch: "[person] blog article"
         │     WebSearch: "site:linkedin.com [person] posts"
         │
         ├─→ Agent 3: FIT + RISK CHECK
         │     Compare company against ICP (from config)
         │     Check competitor match (from config)
         │     Check prior emails (nurge emails list)
         │     WebSearch: "[company] vs [our product]"
         │
         ├─→ Agent 4: CONTACT INFO
         │     WebSearch: "[person] email [company]"
         │     WebFetch: company /contact or /mentions-legales or /impressum
         │     WebSearch: "site:contactout.com [person]"
         │     WebSearch: "site:rocketreach.co [person]"
         │
         └─→ Agent 5: TIMING SIGNALS
               WebSearch: "[company] Q1 2026 plans"
               WebSearch: "[competitor of their current tool] issues"
               Check fiscal year / industry events
```

### All agents return → Combine into Dossier

```
Prospect Dossier: Jane Smith, CTO @ Acme Corp
──────────────────────────────────────────────
FIT:      Strong match (Series B, 120 employees, SaaS, US)
RISK:     None detected (not a competitor, no prior contact)
BUYING:   Just raised $15M Series B (2 months ago), hiring 3 engineers
TIMING:   New CTO (started 4 months ago), fiscal year starts Jan
PERSONA:  Spoke at DevConf 2025 on "scaling engineering teams",
          posts about developer productivity, values transparency
CONTACT:  jane@acme.com (from careers page), LinkedIn: /in/janesmith
ANGLE:    Reference her DevConf talk on scaling → your product helps
          engineering teams ship faster → relevant to her $15M growth mandate

CONFIDENCE: High (multiple corroborating sources)
```

### Present to user for review before drafting.

---

## Signal Sources Summary

| Source | Free? | What It Gets You | How to Access |
|--------|-------|-----------------|---------------|
| **Google (WebSearch)** | Yes | Everything — the starting point for all signals | WebSearch tool |
| **Company website** | Yes | Product info, team, careers, legal pages (contact info) | WebFetch tool |
| **LinkedIn posts** | Yes | What prospects think about, recent activity | WebSearch: "site:linkedin.com [person]" |
| **LinkedIn profile** | Blocked | Direct scrape fails (403/999) | Use web search to get profile snippets |
| **Crunchbase** | Partial | Funding, investors, company info | WebSearch (basic), WebFetch (some pages) |
| **G2** | Yes | Reviews, competitor comparisons, pain signals | WebSearch: "site:g2.com [company]" |
| **Glassdoor** | Yes | Company culture, leadership sentiment | WebSearch: "site:glassdoor.com [company]" |
| **GitHub** | Yes | Tech stack, open source activity, contributor profiles | WebSearch/WebFetch |
| **Product Hunt** | Yes | Product launches, tech adoption | WebSearch: "site:producthunt.com [company]" |
| **Twitter/X** | Partial | Real-time signals, opinions, announcements | WebSearch: "site:twitter.com [person/company]" |
| **News (TechCrunch, etc.)** | Yes | Funding, launches, leadership changes | WebSearch |
| **Job boards** | Yes | Hiring signals, tech stack from requirements | WebSearch, WebFetch careers pages |
| **SEC/EDGAR** | Yes | Revenue, financials (public companies only) | WebSearch, WebFetch |
| **Mentions Légales** | Yes | Contact info (French companies, legally required) | WebFetch: [company].fr/mentionslegales |
| **Impressum** | Yes | Contact info (German companies, legally required) | WebFetch: [company].de/impressum |
| **ContactOut** | Paid | Verified email + phone from LinkedIn URL | Future connector |
| **Apollo** | Freemium | Email, company data, technographics | Future connector |
| **Clay** | Paid | Waterfall enrichment from multiple sources | Future connector |
| **BuiltWith** | Freemium | Technology stack detection | WebFetch (basic), future connector |

---

## How Skills Use Signals

| Skill | Which Signals | How |
|-------|--------------|-----|
| **nurge-research** | All of them — builds the full dossier | Dispatches parallel agents per category |
| **nurge-qualify** | Fit + Risk + Buying | Scores against ICP, checks competitor, checks BANT |
| **nurge-outbound** | Persona + Timing + Buying | Uses signals to personalize the email angle |
| **nurge-sequence** | Timing | Determines sequence cadence and urgency |
| **nurge-followup** | Buying + Timing | References trigger events in follow-ups |
| **nurge-review** | All (aggregated) | Surfaces which signal types correlate with replies |
